steps.title: Identify the key claim about nature
steps.content: The question specifies that Lowell describes an element of nature as an active participant in the experience—meaning nature must be shown doing something, performing an action, not merely existing in the scene.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A — "Through the water the moon writes her legends / In light, on the smooth, wet sand." shows the moon “writing her legends / In light,” presenting the moon as an active agent that shapes the experience.

Choices B, C, and D present nature as passive (rippling), comparative, or unchanging rather than actively engaging with the scene.

That matches Choice A — "Through the water the moon writes her legends / In light, on the smooth, wet sand.".
